source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, the distance from the midpoint \(M\) of one side of the rhombus \(ABCD\) to the intersection point \(O\) of the diagonals is \(5 \, \text{cm}\). What is the perimeter of the rhombus \(ABCD\)?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
Solution: Since quadrilateral ABCD is a rhombus,\\
it follows that $\angle AOB=90^\circ$,\\
since M is the midpoint of side AB,\\
it follows that AB=2OM=10,\\
therefore, the perimeter of rhombus ABCD is $10\times 4=\boxed{40}$.

image_dependency: Question explicitly says 'As shown in the figure,' indicating the figure is part of the problem statement.
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false both point to strong visual dependence.
The geometric roles of M (midpoint on a side) and O (intersection of diagonals) are tied to the diagram rather than fully specified by text alone.
asset_registry_record shows one image exists and registry_passed=true, so the visual asset is available.
multi_step: Solving requires using at least two geometry relations: a rhombus property involving diagonals and the midpoint relation on a side.
The provided answer uses a chain of reasoning: infer a right angle at O, derive AB=2OM=10, then compute perimeter as 4 times the side length.
This is not a direct lookup; it combines diagram interpretation with geometric deduction and arithmetic.
verifiability: The final answer is a single numeric perimeter value (40), which is easy to check.
The provided solution contains explicit intermediate claims that can be audited step by step against geometry rules and the figure.
answer_present=true and registry_passed=true support downstream checking.
Because the image is available, later evaluators can verify whether the stated diagram relationships match the visual.
